subject: Geography
chapter: Geographical Perspective on Selected Issues and Problems

concepts.title: UN Sustainable Development Goals
concepts.description: The UN SDGs are a set of 17 goals to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all. They cover areas like no poverty, zero hunger, good health, quality education, clean water, and more.

concepts.title: Globalization
concepts.description: Globalization refers to the increasing interconnectedness of the world's economies, societies, and cultures. It has led to the growth of international trade, migration, and exchange of ideas.

concepts.title: Impact of Urbanization
concepts.description: Urbanization has led to rapid growth of cities, increased population density, and strain on infrastructure. It also leads to issues like air and water pollution, housing shortages, and social inequality.

concepts.title: Five Themes of Geography
concepts.description: To remember the five themes of geography - Places, Human Environment Interaction, Movement, Regions, and Flows - use the mnemonic 'PHMRF'.

concepts.title: Misconception about Population Growth Rate
concepts.description: Students often mistake the population growth rate with the birth rate. The population growth rate is the rate at which the total population of a country increases, whereas the birth rate is the number of live births per 1000 people per year.

concepts.title: Role of NGOs in Sustainable Development
concepts.description: Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) play a crucial role in promoting sustainable development by raising awareness, advocating for policy changes, and implementing projects at the grassroots level.

concepts.title: Importance of Regional Planning
concepts.description: Regional planning is essential for sustainable development as it helps in optimal utilization of resources, management of environmental issues, and promotion of economic growth.

concepts.title: Impact of Climate Change on Water Resources
concepts.description: Climate change affects water resources by altering precipitation patterns, increasing evaporation rates, and melting glaciers. This leads to droughts, floods, and water scarcity, ultimately affecting human settlements and ecosystems.
